rulings.Case Title: LORI WILLIAMS VS. WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgemtn, Motion For Judgment Notwithstanding The Verdict Under Ccp 663a. Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF LORI WILLIAMS' Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment, Motion For Judgment Notwithstanding The Verdict Under Ccp 663a and Motion For Reconsideration. Denied. Although titled a "Motion to Set Aside and Vacate Judgment, Motion for Judgment Notwithstanding the Verdict Under CCP § 663a, Motion for Reconsideration," Plaintiff Lori Williams essentially moves for reconsideration of the Order on Motion to Quash Service of Summons and Complaint issued on February 20, 2014. Plaintiff has not demonstrate that there are any new facts or law exist to warrant reconsideration pursuant to CCP § 1008. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks relief from judgment, the requested relief is premature; no judgment has been entered. rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Written Discovery, Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PETER BOWES' Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elan Lambert, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are instructed to provide courtesy copies of all papers filed in support and opposition to the motion: (1) electronically to elan.lambert@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties no later April 11, 2014 at 5 pm; and (2) deliver hard copies to the clerk prior the hearing at 9 am. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE WANG VS. REES SCIENTIFIC CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ELAINE WANG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was mutual consent to the release. The test for mutual consent is "determined by objective, rather than subjective criteria, the test being what the outward manifestations of consent would lead a reasonable person to believe." (Weddington Productions Inc. v. Flick. (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 811.) Here, the evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff knew she was releasing all claims in exchange for one month of severance pay or whether she thought she had agreed to negotiate her severance package. (Disputed Material Facts 8, 12, 13.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ELAINE WANG VS. REES SCIENTIFIC CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT REES SCIENTIFIC CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication. Denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was mutual consent to the release. The test for mutual consent is "determined by objective, rather than subjective criteria, the test being what the outward manifestations of consent would lead a reasonable person to believe." (Weddington Productions Inc. v. Flick. (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 811.) Here, the evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether a reasonable person would believe that Plaintiff thought she released all claims at the time she signed the document. Plaintiff's subsequent communications with Defendant's employees indicate that Plaintiff thought she had agreed to negotiate her severance package. (PSF 3.8, 6.1, 6.3, 6.5.) There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant had a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for terminating Plaintiff's employment. Defendant met its burden by providing evidence showing that Plaintiff was terminated because she did not follow management's advice, she spent too much time in the office instead of with potential customers, and she failed to properly input the meetings she attended in the computer system. (UMF 38, 40.) Plaintiff's evidence shows that Defendant prioritized sales numbers, and her sales numbers were significantly better than the Caucasian male who replaced her. (PSF 35.1, 35.2, 35.5, 35.7, 35.8, 35.9, 40.1, 40.5, 47.1.) Plaintiff's evidence also shows that her performance as a sales representative was praised by her manager and she received an achievement award at the National Sales Meeting. (PSF 19.6.) Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff was terminated because of legitimate non-discriminatory reasons. Additionally, Plaintiff's evidence overcomes the same actor inference. "A strong inference is just that - an inference. The fact that a juror could reasonably draw a different inference is sufficient to preclude summary judgment." (Sandell v. Taylor-Listug, Inc. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 297, 324.) Here, given the evidence showing the emphasis Defendant put on sales numbers, as well as the evidence showing that Plaintiff significantly outperformed her replacement, a juror could reasonably draw a different inference as to why Plaintiff was terminated. Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Rick Saunders, a Regional Manager, initiated the process of Mr. Bussolini replacing Plaintiff because of her gender. (PSF 56.1-56.9.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HUA LIN VS. DALY CITY HB INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judgmnt On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB INC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted as to Plaintiff's second cause of action for violation of Labor Code §510, third cause of action for violation of Labor Code §226.7, fourth cause of action for violation of Labor Code §226, fifth cause of action for violation of Business & Professions Code §17203, and sixth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Each of Plaintiff's abovementioned causes of action are barred by the general release provision of the signed settlement agreement. (Third Amended Complaint, Ex. B.) Moreover, "nonemployee compensation" is not ambiguous. Even if it were ambiguous, Plaintiff has not alleged any physical injury or sickness arising from his employment with Defendant to make emotional distress payments nontaxable. Plaintiff has not alleged specific facts to show that Defendant misrepresented anything to state a cause of action for fraud or declaratory relief. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend because Plaintiff has had four opportunities to allege sufficient facts but has failed to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: 1238 SUTTER STREET, LLC VS. GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, April 14, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANTs GAEHWILER CONSTRUCTION CO. and MARTIN GAEHWILER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e).) The fraudulent concealment claim is not clearly barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP § 337.15(f).) Plaintiff 1238 Sutter Street, LLC has pled sufficient facts to allege a duty to disclose pursuant to Civ. Code § 1711. As Defendants were the general contractor on the subject property, the allegations indicate "that 'the defendant must necessarily possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy' [Citations.]" (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 158.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
